events.label: reasoning
events.text:
We need answer JSON only. Need identify component/condition: GEC spliced improperly. Residential grounding electrode system. Defect cues: splice not irreversible compression or exothermic welding, maybe wire nut/twist splice, not accessible, not listed, not reconnected to grounding electrode conductor without proper splice. NEC 250.64(C) says grounding electrode conductor shall be spliced only by irreversible compression-type connectors listed as grounding and bonding equipment or by exothermic welding. Also must be securely fastened, protected, etc. 250.68 connections. So decision fail, risk high? Improper GEC splice can compromise grounding, shock/fire. Work stage rough-complete. component_conditions installed-defective, non-compliant. percent_complete maybe 80? value 0. findings: GEC splice made with improper method (e.g., wire nut/twist), not listed irreversible compression or exothermic weld; splice inaccessible/not in junction box; potential high resistance/discontinuity. actions: remove improper splice; install listed irreversible compression connector or exothermic weld; ensure splice accessible and bonding integrity; retest continuity/resistance. rationale: NEC requires GEC splices only by listed irreversible compression connectors or exothermic welding; improper splice creates unreliable grounding path. references: NEC 250.64(C), 250.68. Need include confidence high. event_time 0, rate 0, sound_source "", order [], workable [].

Need ensure JSON valid. No prose outside.

    "Splice is not a listed irreversible compression-type grounding/bonding connector or exothermic weld",
    "Improper splice creates a high-resistance or unreliable grounding electrode conductor path"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Remove the improper GEC splice",
    "Install a listed irreversible compression connector rated for grounding and bonding or make an exothermic weld",
    "Ensure the splice is accessible and provides a continuous, low-impedance grounding path",
    "Verify continuity and grounding electrode system resistance before closing up"
  ],
  "rationale": "NEC 250.64(C) requires grounding electrode conductor splices to be made only with listed irreversible compression connectors or exothermic welding. An improper splice violates this requirement and can compromise the effectiveness of the grounding electrode system, creating shock and fire hazards.",
  "references": [
    "NEC 250.64(C)",
    "NEC 250.68"
  ]
